sn35.56-59:1.1:
… "Venerable sir, how should one know, how should one see, for the taints to be abandoned? … for the taints to be uprooted? … for the underlying tendencies to be abandoned? … for the underlying tendencies to be uprooted?"
"Bhikkhu, when one knows and sees the eye as nonself, the underlying tendencies are uprooted. When one knows and sees forms as nonself …
… When one knows and sees thus, bhikkhu, the underlying tendencies are uprooted."
